creator: yanefbsd@gmail.com
text:
On Wed, Jun 9, 2010 at 1:48 PM, Giovanni Trematerra
<giovanni.trematerra@gmail.com> wrote:
> Sorry, I missed to add you in Cc.
> Just FYI
>
>
> ---------- Forwarded message ----------
> From: Giovanni Trematerra <giovanni.trematerra@gmail.com>
> Date: Wed, Jun 9, 2010 at 10:46 PM
> Subject: Re: kern/147695: [PATCH] src/sys/dev/twa update
> To: d@delphij.net
> Cc: delphij@freebsd.org
>
>
> On Wed, Jun 9, 2010 at 10:37 PM, Xin LI <delphij@delphij.net> wrote:
>> -----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
>> Hash: SHA256
>>
>> On 2010/06/09 02:50, Giovanni Trematerra wrote:
>>> The following reply was made to PR kern/147695; it has been noted by GN=
ATS.
>>>
>>> From: Giovanni Trematerra <giovanni.trematerra@gmail.com>
>>> To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org, tom.couch@lsi.com
>>> Cc: Garrett Cooper <yanefbsd@gmail.com>
>>> Subject: Re: kern/147695: [PATCH] src/sys/dev/twa update
>>> Date: Wed, 9 Jun 2010 11:25:18 +0200
>>>
>>> =A0You should also test this patch against PR kern/145510.
>>> =A0If the patch resolve that issue PR 145510 need to be closed.
>>
>> It must solved the issue.
>>
>> + =A0 =A0 =A0 if (ctlr->reset_in_progress || !(ctlr->active))
>> + =A0 =A0 =A0 =A0 =A0 =A0 =A0 goto out;
>>
>
> When I first tried to solve that issue, I used a way like this but
> Garret reported
> on -current:
> "The only problem is that bootup is really wonky now,
> because the RAID had a LOT of issues attaching to cam(4) (failed in
> 2/3 cold boot attempts);"
>
> So I wonder if there are still some ill-effects.
> Anyway if all it's ok, 145510 need to be closed.

I need to reinstall CURRENT on that machine because I wiped it out and
installed Windows (for other work reasons).

I'll look into this, but it's going to take me a couple of weeks
because of more pressing stuff on my plate.

Thanks though for the heads up :),
-Garrett
